page: Microsoft Azure B16ms is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs with 1.0 thread per core. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 131 GB of local storage.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile characterized by weak single-core capabilities but average multi-core and memory performance. Single-core tests, including stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU score fall into the middle 50% average range. Memory bandwidth tests, both cached and uncached, also demonstrate average performance, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 97.22 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the instance achieves average throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving, though text generation for small language models is poor, placing in the bottom 10%.

The burstable CPU allocation model offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable resource demands, allowing users to pay for lower baseline performance while retaining the ability to burst to full CPU utilization. However, the tradeoff is the weak single-threaded performance and the potential depletion of CPU credits during sustained high-utilization periods. This server is suited for workloads such as database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks that do not require continuous maximum CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure B16ms is a burstable virtual machine from the BS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM and 131 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show weak single-core performance, placing in the bottom 25%, but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities in the middle 50% tier. The burstable CPU allocation model provides a cost-efficient resource density for workloads with fluctuating CPU demands, though it presents a tradeoff in sustained single-threaded execution. It is suitable for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A burstable virtual machine combining sixteen Intel Xeon cores and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for variable, multi-threaded workloads.
